acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Canonical local store with configured `origin` and missing remote store ref publishes `refs/heads/beads/store` on `bd migrate to 1`.
- [ ] The command remains a local no-op (`commit_oid: null`) in that case.
- [ ] `push` disposition is truthful for origin-present vs origin-missing cases.
- [ ] Integration tests cover the case.
description:
**Problem**
`bd migrate to 1` still treats a canonical local store plus configured `origin` with no remote `refs/heads/beads/store` as a no-op with `push: skipped_no_remote`. That leaves the remote store ref absent even though the repo has an origin and a canonical local store ready to publish.

The design matrix says case 2 (`local present`, `remote missing`) must migrate from local only, parent on `local_oid`, and use the same remote-aware execute contract as other cases. A canonical store in that case should still publish the local store ref to origin instead of silently skipping push.

**Files:** `crates/beads-git/src/sync.rs`, `crates/beads-rs/tests/integration/cli/migration.rs`, `MIGRATION.md`
design:
**Design**
Treat `origin exists but remote store ref is missing` as a publishable case even when the merged snapshot is already canonical.

- Keep the no-op local rewrite behavior: do not mint a new commit if the local tree already matches the canonical snapshot.
- If `origin` exists and `refs/remotes/origin/beads/store` is missing, push the existing local `refs/heads/beads/store` to create the remote store ref unless `--no-push` was requested.
- Reserve `skipped_no_remote` for repositories without an `origin` remote.
- Add integration coverage for canonical local/no-remote-ref execution and make the docs describe the truthful push outcome.
title: Canonical local/no-remote migration should publish origin store ref

acceptance_criteria: - [ ] CBOR encode/decode roundtrip test for LabelAdd/LabelRemove.\n- [ ] CBOR encode/decode roundtrip test for DepAdd/DepRemove.\n- [ ] Test: multiple LabelAdd with same (bead_id,label) but different dots are preserved.
description:
**Problem**
No explicit CBOR roundtrip tests for new label/dep ops; regressions could break WAL/replication.

**Files**: src/core/event.rs, tests/integration/fixtures/event_body.rs
design: **Design**\nAdd tests that encode EventBody with LabelAdd/LabelRemove and DepAdd/DepRemove, then decode and re-encode to assert byte-for-byte equivalence and semantic equality.\nInclude multiple LabelAdd ops with same label but different dots to ensure TxnOpKey dot inclusion is preserved.
title: CBOR roundtrip tests for label/dep OR-Set ops
